title: aglaia dubia
intro: Aglaia dubia, also known as the "bitter moon" tree, is a species of plant in the Meliaceae family, native to Southeast Asia. The bark and roots of the Aglaia dubia plant contain medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and fight off infections, providing health benefits such as improved immune function and relief from pain. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark and roots of the Aglaia dubia plant are typically made into a decoction or infused in hot water to be consumed as a tea, or used in combination with other herbs to create a medicinal tonic.
